description: Manual validation register. event_id = <city_id>:<date>:<category>. Status: validated (independent observation confirms the condition), corroborated (credible news/forecast consistent, no official measurement), unverified (searched, no public record found; an honest null), contradicted (independent evidence disagrees). Events absent from this file are simply not yet researched. Misses and nulls are kept visible: this is the tool's published evidence log. As of 2026-07-08, 51 events checked across 19 cities: 5 validated, 20 corroborated, 26 no public record, 0 contradicted. Station readings quoted in press differ from ERA5 grid-cell values by 1–5 units in several cases; that is the documented station-vs-grid-cell discrepancy rather than contradiction.

events.note: Kashmir InFocus (Jan 2026): heavy Western Disturbance hit J&K on the evening of Jan 22 with peak impact Jan 23; Sonamarg recorded 'heavy snowfall of over two feet', avalanches, stranded vehicles, road closed by authorities.

Media India (Jan 2026): same Jan 22-23 storm: 'snowfall, avalanches strike Sonamarg', roads blocked, avalanche burying buildings/vehicles; consistent with a very heavy precipitation day (no separate mm measurement found).

Outlook India: higher reaches of Kashmir received snowfall on Dec 21, 2025 at the start of Chillai-Kalan; officials confirmed ongoing snowfall at Sonamarg that day; ~2-8 inches at Gulmarg, 6 inches at Sadhna Top.

Deccan Herald (Aug 31, 2025): 'rare late-summer weather event': Sonamarg and Zojila 'received nearly a foot of snow'; residents worried about premature snow onset. Matches the flagged late-August unseasonal snow episode.

Searched Kashmir cold wave October 2025 / Sonamarg minimum temperature mid-October 2025: found an early-October (Oct 3-8) cold snap with no temperature figures, and dated Sonamarg readings (-5.8 to -12.5°C) only for Dec 2025-Jan 2026. Nothing for Oct 12 specifically; the numerically close -8.3°C press figure is from Jan 19, 2026 and was not stretched to cover this event.
Deccan Herald, citing IMD: Kolkata's season-low minimum of 11°C on Dec 31, 2025 (2.8°C below normal), cold snap continuing into early January. ERA5 grid-cell value 9.6°C; ~1.4°C station-vs-cell gap.

Daily Jagran, citing an IMD yellow alert: heavy rainfall of 7-11 cm forecast for Kolkata for Jul 29, 2025, with active monsoon rain confirmed the day before. Forecast-consistent; no official post-event observed total found.

Skymet (Jul 3, 2025): heavy-to-very-heavy rain and flood risk forecast for Bhopal and surrounding MP districts specifically for Jul 5-8, 2025. Forecast-consistent; no exact observed mm figure located.

Free Press Journal (Aug 8-15, 2025): roughly two-week monsoon-break/dry spell in MP with Bhopal receiving only light rain through Aug 14-15. Supports the dry-spell explanation for the warm anomaly: a seasonal-percentile exceedance rather than a newsworthy heatwave.

OMMCOM News, citing IMD: Bhubaneswar's coldest November night in 25 years (12.9°C) on Nov 16, 2025, IMD Yellow Warning for Khurda, cold conditions through Nov 18. ERA5 cell 14.6-15.0°C; station-vs-cell gap ~2°C.

Multiple outlets: unusual early-March 2026 heat surge in the Pune/Mumbai region; IMD advisory (Mar 7) noting the region peaked near 38.9°C, ~6°C above seasonal average. No source pins Mar 8 at Pune specifically.

Skymet, citing IMD station data: Pune fell to 9.4°C on Nov 18, 2025 (second-lowest that day), part of the season's first cold wave alongside Nashik at 9.2°C. Within ±1 day of the flagged sequence (Nov 16-19).

Deccan Herald (Dec 12, 2025), citing IMD: Bengaluru entering its 'coldest December phase in recent years', 12-14°C minimums forecast through the following week (spanning Dec 20). No exact date/value match for Dec 20.

Siasat/DNA India: Dec 12, 2025 reported as Hyderabad's coldest day in 7 years, with station readings including University of Hyderabad 6.3°C and Jeedimetla 11°C. Exact date match, IMD-sourced figures.

Searched Bengaluru cool/cloudy weather, heavy rain, waterlogging, and IMD alerts around Aug 25, 2025; found only general August climate averages and unrelated dated rain events (July, Oct 2025). A cloudy monsoon cool day plausibly generates no coverage; the flag remains reanalysis-only.
Searched news + weather archives for Delhi 9 Oct 2023 heat; Oct 2023 monthly max ~35.4°C but nothing confirms ~36.5°C on that date. No contradiction.
Skymet reported Delhi's coldest morning of the season at 10.4°C on 10 Nov 2025, consistent with the flagged ~10.7°C on 11 Nov.
Weather Spark logs a thunderstorm with rain at Delhi IGI on 2 Mar 2024; Deccan Herald reports a Delhi-NCR hailstorm on 3 Mar 2024 (unseasonal March rain).
Searched for Mumbai warm/humid conditions on 1 Sep 2023; found only September seasonal averages, no date-specific report.
SCMP, Gulf News and others report heavy monsoon rain battering Mumbai on 8–9 Sep 2023, a fatal Mankhurd building collapse and 200mm+ in 24h.
Free Press Journal / Outlook report a mid-Dec 2024 Mumbai cold spell (Santacruz ~14°C on 16 Dec, season's coldest mornings), consistent with a cool ~16.8°C around 17 Dec.
AccuWeather historical data shows 45.6°C at Jaipur on 28 May 2024 (one day after the flag); IMD-quoted reports document a late-May 2024 Rajasthan heatwave.
Searched Jaipur Oct 2025 early cold; typical October lows near 15.5°C consistent with the flag but no source confirms 9 Oct 2025 specifically.
Searched Jaipur rainfall 16 Sep 2023; only regional IMD data (Sep 2023 ~113% of normal nationally), no city/date-specific figure.
Lokmat Times and other outlets document a severe late-May 2024 Rajasthan heatwave with Jodhpur ~44–46°C, consistent with the flagged ~46.1°C.
Searched Jodhpur / Rajasthan unseasonable cold Oct 2025 and date variants; no news, weather report or IMD data found.
Found IMD bulletins on the monsoon's northern limit near Jodhpur around 8–9 Jul 2023 but no confirmation of ~23mm daily rainfall on that date.
Searched Chandigarh late-July 2024 heat; no date-specific corroboration found.

A subagent proposed 'contradicted' from May-2024 being rainless with 46°C peak heat, but the flag is a cool NIGHT: our data has 2 May 2024 at min 15.5°C / max 33.4°C / 0mm: a clear dry night rather than rain-cooled, and consistent with a pre-heatwave airmass. No direct evidence on the night min itself, so recorded as no-public-record rather than contradicted.
ETV Bharat (9 Jul 2023) reports Chandigarh recorded 322.2mm in 24h, breaking a 23-year IMD record, in the major July 2023 north-India flood event.
Searched Lucknow warm day 2 Sep 2023; no date-specific report found.
Searched Lucknow cold 10 Feb 2024; no date-specific report found.
Searched Lucknow heavy rain 28 Aug 2024; no date-specific report found.
IMD's official account noted Ahmedabad at 45.5°C on 24 May 2024 with a heatwave alert; press put 23 May at 46.6–46.7°C, hottest in 8 years, red alert in force.
DeshGujarat (2 Mar 2024) reports unseasonal rain and strong winds over Ahmedabad from a cyclonic circulation, a plausible cooling trigger ~2 days before the flagged 4 Mar reading.
Outlook reports Ahmedabad got 76mm in 12h to 6pm on 18 Sep 2023 with flooding and evacuations under an IMD red alert; magnitude differs from the flag (likely a different station/window).
Cyclone Michaung's heaviest Chennai rain was 2–4 Dec 2023, weakening toward Andhra by 6 Dec, so nothing contradicts a warm 6 Dec, but no source positively confirms it. Left as no-public-record.

IMD Chennai's extended-range bulletin shows Tamil Nadu at large rainfall excess for 7–13 May 2026 with rain on 9–13 May, consistent with a rain-suppressed cool max ~12 May (sub-division rather than station level).
Searched Chennai rain 18 Sep 2023; no date-specific report found.
Weather.com's dated 7 Oct 2023 forecast warned of isolated heavy-to-very-heavy rain (64.5–115.5mm) over Assam and Meghalaya for 7–8 Oct from a decaying low, matching the flag's region and date.
Searched; no date-specific report for a warm 1 Aug 2023 in Guwahati.
A cool monsoon day; searched, no public coverage found.
Searched; the well-documented 3–5 Oct 2023 disaster was the Sikkim GLOF / North Bengal floods ~500km away, which does not match Guwahati, so no corroboration claimed.

A documented Darjeeling snowfall/cold event was ~17 Jan 2024, 10 days off the flagged 27 Jan, so not counted; no date-matched source found.
